instruction:
We now define an algorithm: The definition of a(n) is the number of internal nodes in a rooted tree whose Matula-Goebel number is n. The Matula-Goebel number of a rooted tree is defined as follows: (1) The one-vertex tree is assigned the number 1; (2) If T1, T2,..., Tk are the branches of the root of tree T and their respective Matula-Goebel numbers are n1, n2,..., nk, then the Matula-Goebel number of T is the product of the primes with indices n1, n2,..., nk. Given the input x_list (a series of values): [91, 92, 93, 94, 95, 96, 97, 98, 99, 100], determine the corresponding output sequence y_list.

Options:
A. [3, 3, 7, 5, 5, 2, 6, 4, 6, 5]
B. [4, 5, 7, 5, 4, 2, 7, 4, 6, 6]
C. [5, 5, 6, 6, 3, 2, 5, 3, 5, 4]
D. [6, 4, 6, 4, 4, 3, 6, 3, 5, 5]
E. [5, 4, 7, 6, 3, 2, 6, 4, 6, 5]
F. [4, 4, 6, 5, 4, 2, 6, 3, 6, 5]
G. [4, 4, 5, 6, 4, 3, 7, 3, 6, 4]
H. [4, 3, 6, 4, 4, 3, 5, 3, 7, 5]
I. [5, 4, 7, 5, 4, 3, 5, 3, 6, 4]
J. [3, 4, 6, 5, 5, 3, 5, 4, 7, 6]
